fusion.source_title: Reciprocal Rank Fusion outperforms Condorcet and individual Rank Learning Methods
fusion.candidate_depth: all schema documents in each component ranking
fusion.tie_break: descending fused score, then ascending document_id
fusion.justification: rank fusion avoids comparing uncalibrated BM25 and cosine scores; equal fixed weights avoid outcome-tuned preference; complete component rankings match the RRF document-set definition and preserve both ranks for every selected hit

dense_component.query_prefix: Represent this sentence for searching relevant passages:

documents.granularity: one table document followed by one document per column
documents.ordering: case-insensitive SQLite catalog-name order, then SQLite pragma_table_info column order

trace_contract.per_hit_components: raw BM25 score, cosine score, component ranks, and weighted reciprocal-rank contributions
trace_contract.embedding_audit: ordered float32 document-matrix and query-vector SHA-256 digests

schema_pack.table_hit: include every column in the selected table
schema_pack.column_hits_only: include selected columns, primary-key columns, and selected-table join endpoints
schema_pack.foreign_keys: include edges whose endpoint tables are selected, in lexicographic order
schema_pack.serialization: one complete table declaration per line followed by FK lines

reproducibility_limitations: CPU and math-library differences can produce small dense-score differences
stable document-ID tie-breaking resolves exact fusion ties but not near-ties that reverse after component rank changes
hosted SQL generation remains nondeterministic and uses committed response recordings for replay

pre_generation_pack_audit.inputs: public questions and database catalogs only
